./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:7:# An example hook script to integrate Watchman
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:8:# (https://facebook.github.io/watchman/) with git to speed up detecting
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:9:# new and modified files.
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:10:#
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:11:# The hook is passed a version (currently 2) and last update token
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:12:# formatted as a string and outputs to stdout a new update token and
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:13:# all files that have been modified since the update token. Paths must
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:14:# be relative to the root of the working tree and separated by a single NUL.
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:15:#
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:16:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "query-watchman" and set
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:17:# 'git config core.fsmonitor .git/hooks/query-watchman'
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:18:#
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:19:my ($version, $last_update_token) = @ARGV;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:21:# Uncomment for debugging
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:22:# print STDERR "$0 $version $last_update_token\n";
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:24:# Check the hook interface version
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:25:if ($version ne 2) {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:26:	die "Unsupported query-fsmonitor hook version '$version'.\n" .
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:27:	    "Falling back to scanning...\n";
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:28:}
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:30:my $git_work_tree = get_working_dir();
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:32:my $retry = 1;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:34:my $json_pkg;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:35:eval {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:36:	require JSON::XS;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:37:	$json_pkg = "JSON::XS";
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:38:	1;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:39:} or do {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:40:	require JSON::PP;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:41:	$json_pkg = "JSON::PP";

./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:46:sub launch_watchman {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:47:	my $o = watchman_query();
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:48:	if (is_work_tree_watched($o)) {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:49:		output_result($o->{clock}, @{$o->{files}});
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:50:	}
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:51:}
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:53:sub output_result {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:54:	my ($clockid, @files) = @_;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:56:	# Uncomment for debugging watchman output
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:57:	# open (my $fh, ">", ".git/watchman-output.out");
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:58:	# binmode $fh, ":utf8";

./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:60:	# close $fh;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:62:	binmode STDOUT, ":utf8";

./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:65:	local $, = "\0";
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:66:	print @files;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:67:}
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:69:sub watchman_clock {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:70:	my $response = qx/watchman clock "$git_work_tree"/;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:71:	die "Failed to get clock id on '$git_work_tree'.\n" .
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:72:		"Falling back to scanning...\n" if $? != 0;

./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:75:}
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:77:sub watchman_query {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:78:	my $pid = open2(\*CHLD_OUT, \*CHLD_IN, 'watchman -j --no-pretty')
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:79:	or die "open2() failed: $!\n" .
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:80:	"Falling back to scanning...\n";
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:82:	# In the query expression below we're asking for names of files that
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:83:	# changed since $last_update_token but not from the .git folder.
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:84:	#
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:85:	# To accomplish this, we're using the "since" generator to use the
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:86:	# recency index to select candidate nodes and "fields" to limit the
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:87:	# output to file names only. Then we're using the "expression" term to
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:88:	# further constrain the results.
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:89:	my $last_update_line = "";
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:90:	if (substr($last_update_token, 0, 1) eq "c") {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:91:		$last_update_token = "\"$last_update_token\"";
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:92:		$last_update_line = qq[\n"since": $last_update_token,];
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:93:	}
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:94:	my $query = <<"	END";
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:95:		["query", "$git_work_tree", {$last_update_line
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:96:			"fields": ["name"],
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:97:			"expression": ["not", ["dirname", ".git"]]
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:98:		}]
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:99:	END
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:101:	# Uncomment for debugging the watchman query
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:102:	# open (my $fh, ">", ".git/watchman-query.json");
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:103:	# print $fh $query;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:104:	# close $fh;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:106:	print CHLD_IN $query;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:107:	close CHLD_IN;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:108:	my $response = do {local $/; <CHLD_OUT>};
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:110:	# Uncomment for debugging the watch response
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:111:	# open ($fh, ">", ".git/watchman-response.json");

./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:120:	return $json_pkg->new->utf8->decode($response);
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:121:}
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:123:sub is_work_tree_watched {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:124:	my ($output) = @_;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:125:	my $error = $output->{error};
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:126:	if ($retry > 0 and $error and $error =~ m/unable to resolve root .* directory (.*) is not watched/) {
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:127:		$retry--;

./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:148:		"Falling back to scanning...\n" if $error;

./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:151:		$last_update_token = $o->{clock};
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:153:		eval { launch_watchman() };
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:154:		return 0;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:155:	}
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:157:	die "Watchman: $error.\n" .
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:158:	"Falling back to scanning...\n" if $error;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:160:	return 1;
./.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample:161:}

./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:2:#
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:3:# An example hook script to make use of push options.
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:4:# The example simply echoes all push options that start with 'echoback='
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:5:# and rejects all pushes when the "reject" push option is used.
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:6:#
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:7:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "pre-receive".
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:9:if test -n "$GIT_PUSH_OPTION_COUNT"
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:10:then
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:11:	i=0
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:12:	while test "$i" -lt "$GIT_PUSH_OPTION_COUNT"
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:13:	do
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:14:		eval "value=\$GIT_PUSH_OPTION_$i"
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:15:		case "$value" in
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:16:		echoback=*)
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:17:			echo "echo from the pre-receive-hook: ${value#*=}" >&2
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:18:			;;
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:19:		reject)
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:20:			exit 1
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:21:		esac
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:22:		i=$((i + 1))
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:23:	done
./.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample:24:fi

./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:3:# An example hook script to validate a patch (and/or patch series) before
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:4:# sending it via email.
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:5:#
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:6:# The hook should exit with non-zero status after issuing an appropriate
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:7:# message if it wants to prevent the email(s) from being sent.
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:8:#
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:9:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "sendemail-validate".
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:10:#
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:11:# By default, it will only check that the patch(es) can be applied on top of
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:12:# the default upstream branch without conflicts in a secondary worktree. After
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:13:# validation (successful or not) of the last patch of a series, the worktree
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:14:# will be deleted.
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:15:#
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:16:# The following config variables can be set to change the default remote and
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:17:# remote ref that are used to apply the patches against:
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:18:#
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:19:#   sendemail.validateRemote (default: origin)
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:20:#   sendemail.validateRemoteRef (default: HEAD)
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:21:#
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:22:# Replace the TODO placeholders with appropriate checks according to your
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:23:# needs.

./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:46:# main -------------------------------------------------------------------------
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:48:if test "$GIT_SENDEMAIL_FILE_COUNTER" = 1
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:49:then
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:50:	remote=$(git config --default origin --get sendemail.validateRemote) &&
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:51:	ref=$(git config --default HEAD --get sendemail.validateRemoteRef) &&

./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:54:	git config --replace-all sendemail.validateWorktree "$worktree"
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:55:else
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:56:	worktree=$(git config --get sendemail.validateWorktree)
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:57:fi || {

./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:59:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:60:}
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:62:unset GIT_DIR GIT_WORK_TREE
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:63:cd "$worktree" &&
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:65:if grep -q "^diff --git " "$1"

./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:72:if test "$GIT_SENDEMAIL_FILE_COUNTER" = "$GIT_SENDEMAIL_FILE_TOTAL"
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:73:then
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:74:	git config --unset-all sendemail.validateWorktree &&
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:75:	trap 'git worktree remove -ff "$worktree"' EXIT &&
./.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample:76:	validate_series

./.git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample:2:#

./.git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample:4:# applypatch from an e-mail message.

./.git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample:6:# The hook should exit with non-zero status after issuing an
./.git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample:7:# appropriate message if it wants to stop the commit.  The hook is
./.git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample:8:# allowed to edit the commit message file.

./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:2:#
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:3:# Copyright (c) 2006, 2008 Junio C Hamano
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:4:#
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:5:# The "pre-rebase" hook is run just before "git rebase" starts doing
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:6:# its job, and can prevent the command from running by exiting with
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:7:# non-zero status.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:8:#
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:9:# The hook is called with the following parameters:
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:10:#
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:11:# $1 -- the upstream the series was forked from.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:12:# $2 -- the branch being rebased (or empty when rebasing the current branch).
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:13:#
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:14:# This sample shows how to prevent topic branches that are already
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:15:# merged to 'next' branch from getting rebased, because allowing it
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:16:# would result in rebasing already published history.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:18:publish=next
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:19:basebranch="$1"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:20:if test "$#" = 2
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:21:then
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:22:	topic="refs/heads/$2"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:23:else
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:24:	topic=`git symbolic-ref HEAD` ||
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:25:	exit 0 ;# we do not interrupt rebasing detached HEAD
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:26:fi
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:28:case "$topic" in
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:29:refs/heads/??/*)
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:30:	;;
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:31:*)
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:32:	exit 0 ;# we do not interrupt others.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:33:	;;
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:34:esac
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:36:# Now we are dealing with a topic branch being rebased
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:37:# on top of master.  Is it OK to rebase it?
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:39:# Does the topic really exist?
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:40:git show-ref -q "$topic" || {
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:41:	echo >&2 "No such branch $topic"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:42:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:43:}
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:45:# Is topic fully merged to master?
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:46:not_in_master=`git rev-list --pretty=oneline ^master "$topic"`
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:47:if test -z "$not_in_master"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:48:then
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:49:	echo >&2 "$topic is fully merged to master; better remove it."
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:50:	exit 1 ;# we could allow it, but there is no point.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:51:fi
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:53:# Is topic ever merged to next?  If so you should not be rebasing it.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:54:only_next_1=`git rev-list ^master "^$topic" ${publish} | sort`
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:55:only_next_2=`git rev-list ^master           ${publish} | sort`
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:56:if test "$only_next_1" = "$only_next_2"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:57:then
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:58:	not_in_topic=`git rev-list "^$topic" master`
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:59:	if test -z "$not_in_topic"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:60:	then
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:61:		echo >&2 "$topic is already up to date with master"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:62:		exit 1 ;# we could allow it, but there is no point.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:63:	else
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:64:		exit 0
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:65:	fi
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:66:else
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:67:	not_in_next=`git rev-list --pretty=oneline ^${publish} "$topic"`
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:68:	/usr/bin/perl -e '
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:69:		my $topic = $ARGV[0];
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:70:		my $msg = "* $topic has commits already merged to public branch:\n";
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:71:		my (%not_in_next) = map {
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:72:			/^([0-9a-f]+) /;
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:73:			($1 => 1);
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:74:		} split(/\n/, $ARGV[1]);
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:75:		for my $elem (map {
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:76:				/^([0-9a-f]+) (.*)$/;
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:77:				[$1 => $2];
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:78:			} split(/\n/, $ARGV[2])) {
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:79:			if (!exists $not_in_next{$elem->[0]}) {
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:80:				if ($msg) {
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:81:					print STDERR $msg;
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:82:					undef $msg;
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:83:				}
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:84:				print STDERR " $elem->[1]\n";
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:85:			}
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:86:		}
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:87:	' "$topic" "$not_in_next" "$not_in_master"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:88:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:89:fi
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:91:<<\DOC_END
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:93:This sample hook safeguards topic branches that have been
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:94:published from being rewound.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:96:The workflow assumed here is:
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:98: * Once a topic branch forks from "master", "master" is never
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:99:   merged into it again (either directly or indirectly).
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:101: * Once a topic branch is fully cooked and merged into "master",
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:102:   it is deleted.  If you need to build on top of it to correct
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:103:   earlier mistakes, a new topic branch is created by forking at
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:104:   the tip of the "master".  This is not strictly necessary, but
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:105:   it makes it easier to keep your history simple.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:107: * Whenever you need to test or publish your changes to topic
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:108:   branches, merge them into "next" branch.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:110:The script, being an example, hardcodes the publish branch name
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:111:to be "next", but it is trivial to make it configurable via
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:112:$GIT_DIR/config mechanism.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:114:With this workflow, you would want to know:
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:116:(1) ... if a topic branch has ever been merged to "next".  Young
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:117:    topic branches can have stupid mistakes you would rather
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:118:    clean up before publishing, and things that have not been
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:119:    merged into other branches can be easily rebased without
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:120:    affecting other people.  But once it is published, you would
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:121:    not want to rewind it.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:123:(2) ... if a topic branch has been fully merged to "master".
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:124:    Then you can delete it.  More importantly, you should not
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:125:    build on top of it -- other people may already want to
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:126:    change things related to the topic as patches against your
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:127:    "master", so if you need further changes, it is better to
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:128:    fork the topic (perhaps with the same name) afresh from the
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:129:    tip of "master".
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:131:Let's look at this example:
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:133:		   o---o---o---o---o---o---o---o---o---o "next"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:134:		  /       /           /           /
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:135:		 /   a---a---b A     /           /
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:136:		/   /               /           /
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:137:	       /   /   c---c---c---c B         /
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:138:	      /   /   /             \         /
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:139:	     /   /   /   b---b C     \       /
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:140:	    /   /   /   /             \     /
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:141:    ---o---o---o---o---o---o---o---o---o---o---o "master"
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:144:A, B and C are topic branches.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:146: * A has one fix since it was merged up to "next".
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:148: * B has finished.  It has been fully merged up to "master" and "next",
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:149:   and is ready to be deleted.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:151: * C has not merged to "next" at all.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:153:We would want to allow C to be rebased, refuse A, and encourage
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:154:B to be deleted.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:156:To compute (1):
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:158:	git rev-list ^master ^topic next
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:159:	git rev-list ^master        next
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:161:	if these match, topic has not merged in next at all.
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:163:To compute (2):
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:165:	git rev-list master..topic
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:167:	if this is empty, it is fully merged to "master".
./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:169:DOC_END
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:2:#
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:3:# An example hook script to verify what is about to be committed.
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:4:# Called by "git merge" with no arguments.  The hook should
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:5:# exit with non-zero status after issuing an appropriate message to
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:6:# stderr if it wants to stop the merge commit.
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:7:#
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:8:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "pre-merge-commit".
./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:10:. git-sh-setup

./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:3:# An example hook script to update a checked-out tree on a git push.
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:4:#
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:5:# This hook is invoked by git-receive-pack(1) when it reacts to git
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:6:# push and updates reference(s) in its repository, and when the push
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:7:# tries to update the branch that is currently checked out and the
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:8:# receive.denyCurrentBranch configuration variable is set to
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:9:# updateInstead.
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:10:#
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:11:# By default, such a push is refused if the working tree and the index
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:12:# of the remote repository has any difference from the currently
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:13:# checked out commit; when both the working tree and the index match
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:14:# the current commit, they are updated to match the newly pushed tip
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:15:# of the branch. This hook is to be used to override the default
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:16:# behaviour; however the code below reimplements the default behaviour
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:17:# as a starting point for convenient modification.
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:18:#
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:19:# The hook receives the commit with which the tip of the current
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:20:# branch is going to be updated:
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:21:commit=$1
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:23:# It can exit with a non-zero status to refuse the push (when it does
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:24:# so, it must not modify the index or the working tree).
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:25:die () {
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:26:	echo >&2 "$*"
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:27:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:28:}
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:30:# Or it can make any necessary changes to the working tree and to the
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:31:# index to bring them to the desired state when the tip of the current
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:32:# branch is updated to the new commit, and exit with a zero status.
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:33:#
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:34:# For example, the hook can simply run git read-tree -u -m HEAD "$1"
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:35:# in order to emulate git fetch that is run in the reverse direction
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:36:# with git push, as the two-tree form of git read-tree -u -m is
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:37:# essentially the same as git switch or git checkout that switches
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:38:# branches while keeping the local changes in the working tree that do
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:39:# not interfere with the difference between the branches.
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:41:# The below is a more-or-less exact translation to shell of the C code
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:42:# for the default behaviour for git's push-to-checkout hook defined in
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:43:# the push_to_deploy() function in builtin/receive-pack.c.
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:44:#
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:45:# Note that the hook will be executed from the repository directory,
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:46:# not from the working tree, so if you want to perform operations on
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:47:# the working tree, you will have to adapt your code accordingly, e.g.
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:48:# by adding "cd .." or using relative paths.
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:50:if ! git update-index -q --ignore-submodules --refresh
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:51:then
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:52:	die "Up-to-date check failed"
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:53:fi
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:55:if ! git diff-files --quiet --ignore-submodules --
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:56:then
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:57:	die "Working directory has unstaged changes"
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:58:fi
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:60:# This is a rough translation of:
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:61:#
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:62:#   head_has_history() ? "HEAD" : EMPTY_TREE_SHA1_HEX
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:63:if git cat-file -e HEAD 2>/dev/null
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:64:then
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:65:	head=HEAD
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:66:else
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:67:	head=$(git hash-object -t tree --stdin </dev/null)
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:68:fi
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:70:if ! git diff-index --quiet --cached --ignore-submodules $head --
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:71:then
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:72:	die "Working directory has staged changes"
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:73:fi
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:75:if ! git read-tree -u -m "$commit"
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:76:then
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:77:	die "Could not update working tree to new HEAD"
./.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample:78:fi
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:2:#
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:3:# An example hook script to verify what is about to be committed.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:4:# Called by "git commit" with no arguments.  The hook should
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:5:# exit with non-zero status after issuing an appropriate message if
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:6:# it wants to stop the commit.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:7:#
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:8:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "pre-commit".
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:10:if git rev-parse --verify HEAD >/dev/null 2>&1
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:11:then
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:12:	against=HEAD
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:13:else
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:14:	# Initial commit: diff against an empty tree object
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:15:	against=$(git hash-object -t tree /dev/null)
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:16:fi
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:18:# If you want to allow non-ASCII filenames set this variable to true.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:19:allownonascii=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allownonascii)
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:21:# Redirect output to stderr.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:22:exec 1>&2
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:24:# Cross platform projects tend to avoid non-ASCII filenames; prevent
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:25:# them from being added to the repository. We exploit the fact that the
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:26:# printable range starts at the space character and ends with tilde.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:27:if [ "$allownonascii" != "true" ] &&
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:28:	# Note that the use of brackets around a tr range is ok here, (it's
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:29:	# even required, for portability to Solaris 10's /usr/bin/tr), since
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:30:	# the square bracket bytes happen to fall in the designated range.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:31:	test $(git diff-index --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A -z $against |
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:32:	  LC_ALL=C tr -d '[ -~]\0' | wc -c) != 0
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:33:then
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:34:	cat <<\EOF
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:35:Error: Attempt to add a non-ASCII file name.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:37:This can cause problems if you want to work with people on other platforms.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:39:To be portable it is advisable to rename the file.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:41:If you know what you are doing you can disable this check using:
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:43:  git config hooks.allownonascii true
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:44:EOF
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:45:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:46:fi
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:48:# If there are whitespace errors, print the offending file names and fail.
./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:49:exec git diff-index --check --cached $against --
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:3:# An example hook script to verify what is about to be pushed.  Called by "git
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:4:# push" after it has checked the remote status, but before anything has been
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:5:# pushed.  If this script exits with a non-zero status nothing will be pushed.
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:6:#
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:7:# This hook is called with the following parameters:
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:8:#
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:9:# $1 -- Name of the remote to which the push is being done
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:10:# $2 -- URL to which the push is being done
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:11:#
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:12:# If pushing without using a named remote those arguments will be equal.
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:13:#
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:14:# Information about the commits which are being pushed is supplied as lines to
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:15:# the standard input in the form:
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:16:#
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:17:#   <local ref> <local oid> <remote ref> <remote oid>
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:18:#
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:19:# This sample shows how to prevent push of commits where the log message starts
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:20:# with "WIP" (work in progress).
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:22:remote="$1"
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:23:url="$2"
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:25:zero=$(git hash-object --stdin </dev/null | tr '[0-9a-f]' '0')
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:27:while read local_ref local_oid remote_ref remote_oid
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:28:do
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:29:	if test "$local_oid" = "$zero"
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:30:	then
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:31:		# Handle delete
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:32:		:
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:33:	else
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:34:		if test "$remote_oid" = "$zero"
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:35:		then
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:36:			# New branch, examine all commits
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:37:			range="$local_oid"
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:38:		else
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:39:			# Update to existing branch, examine new commits
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:40:			range="$remote_oid..$local_oid"
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:41:		fi
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:43:		# Check for WIP commit
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:44:		commit=$(git rev-list -n 1 --grep '^WIP' "$range")
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:45:		if test -n "$commit"
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:46:		then
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:47:			echo >&2 "Found WIP commit in $local_ref, not pushing"
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:48:			exit 1
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:49:		fi
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:50:	fi
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:51:done
./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:53:exit 0
./.git/hooks/update.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/update.sample:2:#
./.git/hooks/update.sample:3:# An example hook script to block unannotated tags from entering.
./.git/hooks/update.sample:4:# Called by "git receive-pack" with arguments: refname sha1-old sha1-new
./.git/hooks/update.sample:5:#
./.git/hooks/update.sample:6:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "update".
./.git/hooks/update.sample:7:#
./.git/hooks/update.sample:8:# Config
./.git/hooks/update.sample:9:# ------
./.git/hooks/update.sample:10:# hooks.allowunannotated
./.git/hooks/update.sample:11:#   This boolean sets whether unannotated tags will be allowed into the
./.git/hooks/update.sample:12:#   repository.  By default they won't be.
./.git/hooks/update.sample:13:# hooks.allowdeletetag
./.git/hooks/update.sample:14:#   This boolean sets whether deleting tags will be allowed in the
./.git/hooks/update.sample:15:#   repository.  By default they won't be.
./.git/hooks/update.sample:16:# hooks.allowmodifytag
./.git/hooks/update.sample:17:#   This boolean sets whether a tag may be modified after creation. By default
./.git/hooks/update.sample:18:#   it won't be.
./.git/hooks/update.sample:19:# hooks.allowdeletebranch
./.git/hooks/update.sample:20:#   This boolean sets whether deleting branches will be allowed in the
./.git/hooks/update.sample:21:#   repository.  By default they won't be.
./.git/hooks/update.sample:22:# hooks.denycreatebranch
./.git/hooks/update.sample:23:#   This boolean sets whether remotely creating branches will be denied
./.git/hooks/update.sample:24:#   in the repository.  By default this is allowed.
./.git/hooks/update.sample:25:#
./.git/hooks/update.sample:27:# --- Command line
./.git/hooks/update.sample:28:refname="$1"
./.git/hooks/update.sample:29:oldrev="$2"
./.git/hooks/update.sample:30:newrev="$3"
./.git/hooks/update.sample:32:# --- Safety check
./.git/hooks/update.sample:33:if [ -z "$GIT_DIR" ]; then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:34:	echo "Don't run this script from the command line." >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:35:	echo " (if you want, you could supply GIT_DIR then run" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:36:	echo "  $0 <ref> <oldrev> <newrev>)" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:37:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:38:fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:40:if [ -z "$refname" -o -z "$oldrev" -o -z "$newrev" ]; then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:41:	echo "usage: $0 <ref> <oldrev> <newrev>" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:42:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:43:fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:45:# --- Config
./.git/hooks/update.sample:46:allowunannotated=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allowunannotated)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:47:allowdeletebranch=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allowdeletebranch)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:48:denycreatebranch=$(git config --type=bool hooks.denycreatebranch)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:49:allowdeletetag=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allowdeletetag)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:50:allowmodifytag=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allowmodifytag)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:52:# check for no description
./.git/hooks/update.sample:53:projectdesc=$(sed -e '1q' "$GIT_DIR/description")
./.git/hooks/update.sample:54:case "$projectdesc" in
./.git/hooks/update.sample:55:"Unnamed repository"* | "")
./.git/hooks/update.sample:56:	echo "*** Project description file hasn't been set" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:57:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:58:	;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:59:esac
./.git/hooks/update.sample:61:# --- Check types
./.git/hooks/update.sample:62:# if $newrev is 0000...0000, it's a commit to delete a ref.
./.git/hooks/update.sample:63:zero=$(git hash-object --stdin </dev/null | tr '[0-9a-f]' '0')
./.git/hooks/update.sample:64:if [ "$newrev" = "$zero" ]; then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:65:	newrev_type=delete
./.git/hooks/update.sample:66:else
./.git/hooks/update.sample:67:	newrev_type=$(git cat-file -t $newrev)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:68:fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:70:case "$refname","$newrev_type" in
./.git/hooks/update.sample:71:	refs/tags/*,commit)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:72:		# un-annotated tag
./.git/hooks/update.sample:73:		short_refname=${refname##refs/tags/}
./.git/hooks/update.sample:74:		if [ "$allowunannotated" != "true" ]; then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:75:			echo "*** The un-annotated tag, $short_refname, is not allowed in this repository" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:76:			echo "*** Use 'git tag [ -a | -s ]' for tags you want to propagate." >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:77:			exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:78:		fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:79:		;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:80:	refs/tags/*,delete)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:81:		# delete tag
./.git/hooks/update.sample:82:		if [ "$allowdeletetag" != "true" ]; then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:83:			echo "*** Deleting a tag is not allowed in this repository" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:84:			exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:85:		fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:86:		;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:87:	refs/tags/*,tag)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:88:		# annotated tag
./.git/hooks/update.sample:89:		if [ "$allowmodifytag" != "true" ] && git rev-parse $refname > /dev/null 2>&1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:90:		then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:91:			echo "*** Tag '$refname' already exists." >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:92:			echo "*** Modifying a tag is not allowed in this repository." >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:93:			exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:94:		fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:95:		;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:96:	refs/heads/*,commit)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:97:		# branch
./.git/hooks/update.sample:98:		if [ "$oldrev" = "$zero" -a "$denycreatebranch" = "true" ]; then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:99:			echo "*** Creating a branch is not allowed in this repository" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:100:			exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:101:		fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:102:		;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:103:	refs/heads/*,delete)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:104:		# delete branch
./.git/hooks/update.sample:105:		if [ "$allowdeletebranch" != "true" ]; then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:106:			echo "*** Deleting a branch is not allowed in this repository" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:107:			exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:108:		fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:109:		;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:110:	refs/remotes/*,commit)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:111:		# tracking branch
./.git/hooks/update.sample:112:		;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:113:	refs/remotes/*,delete)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:114:		# delete tracking branch
./.git/hooks/update.sample:115:		if [ "$allowdeletebranch" != "true" ]; then
./.git/hooks/update.sample:116:			echo "*** Deleting a tracking branch is not allowed in this repository" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:117:			exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:118:		fi
./.git/hooks/update.sample:119:		;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:120:	*)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:121:		# Anything else (is there anything else?)
./.git/hooks/update.sample:122:		echo "*** Update hook: unknown type of update to ref $refname of type $newrev_type" >&2
./.git/hooks/update.sample:123:		exit 1
./.git/hooks/update.sample:124:		;;
./.git/hooks/update.sample:125:esac
./.git/hooks/update.sample:127:# --- Finished
./.git/hooks/update.sample:128:exit 0
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:2:#
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:3:# An example hook script to check the commit log message.
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:4:# Called by "git commit" with one argument, the name of the file
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:5:# that has the commit message.  The hook should exit with non-zero
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:6:# status after issuing an appropriate message if it wants to stop the
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:7:# commit.  The hook is allowed to edit the commit message file.
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:8:#
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:9:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "commit-msg".
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:11:# Uncomment the below to add a Signed-off-by line to the message.
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:12:# Doing this in a hook is a bad idea in general, but the prepare-commit-msg
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:13:# hook is more suited to it.
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:14:#
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:15:# SOB=$(git var GIT_AUTHOR_IDENT | sed -n 's/^\(.*>\).*$/Signed-off-by: \1/p')
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:16:# grep -qs "^$SOB" "$1" || echo "$SOB" >> "$1"
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:18:# This example catches duplicate Signed-off-by lines.
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:20:test "" = "$(grep '^Signed-off-by: ' "$1" |
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:21:	 sort | uniq -c | sed -e '/^[ 	]*1[ 	]/d')" || {
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:22:	echo >&2 Duplicate Signed-off-by lines.
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:23:	exit 1
./.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample:24:}
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:1:#!/bin/sh
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:2:#
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:3:# An example hook script to prepare the commit log message.
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:4:# Called by "git commit" with the name of the file that has the
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:5:# commit message, followed by the description of the commit
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:6:# message's source.  The hook's purpose is to edit the commit
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:7:# message file.  If the hook fails with a non-zero status,
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:8:# the commit is aborted.
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:9:#
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:10:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "prepare-commit-msg".
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:12:# This hook includes three examples. The first one removes the
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:13:# "# Please enter the commit message..." help message.
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:14:#
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:15:# The second includes the output of "git diff --name-status -r"
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:16:# into the message, just before the "git status" output.  It is
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:17:# commented because it doesn't cope with --amend or with squashed
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:18:# commits.
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:19:#
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:20:# The third example adds a Signed-off-by line to the message, that can
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:21:# still be edited.  This is rarely a good idea.
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:23:COMMIT_MSG_FILE=$1
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:24:COMMIT_SOURCE=$2
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:25:SHA1=$3
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:27:/usr/bin/perl -i.bak -ne 'print unless(m/^. Please enter the commit message/..m/^#$/)' "$COMMIT_MSG_FILE"
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:29:# case "$COMMIT_SOURCE,$SHA1" in
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:30:#  ,|template,)
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:31:#    /usr/bin/perl -i.bak -pe '
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:32:#       print "\n" . `git diff --cached --name-status -r`
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:33:# 	 if /^#/ && $first++ == 0' "$COMMIT_MSG_FILE" ;;
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:34:#  *) ;;
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:35:# esac
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:37:# SOB=$(git var GIT_COMMITTER_IDENT | sed -n 's/^\(.*>\).*$/Signed-off-by: \1/p')
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:38:# git interpret-trailers --in-place --trailer "$SOB" "$COMMIT_MSG_FILE"
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:39:# if test -z "$COMMIT_SOURCE"
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:40:# then
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:41:#   /usr/bin/perl -i.bak -pe 'print "\n" if !$first_line++' "$COMMIT_MSG_FILE"
./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:42:# fi

./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:3:# An example hook script to verify what is about to be committed
./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:4:# by applypatch from an e-mail message.
./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:5:#
./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:6:# The hook should exit with non-zero status after issuing an
./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:7:# appropriate message if it wants to stop the commit.
./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:8:#
./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:9:# To enable this hook, rename this file to "pre-applypatch".
./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:11:. git-sh-setup

./README.md:6:Formado por 
./README.md:8:-Daniel Camilo Cedeño Chavarro 
./README.md:10:-Julián Felipe Africano Preciado 
./README.md:12:-Juliana Aguirre Ballesteros 
./README.md:14:-Luna Valentina Leon Gonzales 
./README.md:16:-Diego Alejandro Melgarejo 
./README.md:18:Marrakesh Juego – Arquitectura de Software
./README.md:20:Descripción
./README.md:22:Marrakesh es una implementación digital del juego de mesa Marrakesh, desarrollada bajo principios de arquitectura por capas. El proyecto busca simular la dinámica estratégica del juego original y con nuevas modificaciones (poderes) asi permitiendo a los usuarios interactuar a través de una interfaz gráfica mientras se gestiona la lógica del juego y la persistencia de datos de forma desacoplada.
./README.md:24:¿De qué trata el juego?
./README.md:26:Marrakesh es un juego de estrategia donde los jugadores compiten por dominar el mercado colocando alfombras en un tablero. En cada turno los jugadores mueven a un personaje llamado Assam, colocan alfombras y gestionan monedas, intentando maximizar su territorio y riqueza, tambien se contara con la modificacion para extender el juego, se agregaron nuevos poderes y modificaciones para que el juego no sea tan monotono y se exploren diferentes modos de juego.
./README.md:28:El objetivo es:
./README.md:30:- Controlar la mayor cantidad de casillas del tablero
./README.md:31:- Obtener más monedas que los demás jugadores
./README.md:32:- Tomar decisiones estratégicas en cada turno
./README.md:33:----------------------------------------------------------------------------
./README.md:34:Arquitectura del Proyecto
./README.md:36:El proyecto está estructurado en una arquitectura por capas, promoviendo separación de responsabilidades:
./README.md:38:```
./README.md:39:com.marrakech.game
./README.md:40:│
./README.md:41:├── application      → Lógica de aplicación (servicios)
./README.md:42:├── domain           → Modelo del dominio (reglas del juego)
./README.md:43:├── infrastructure   → Persistencia y acceso a datos
./README.md:44:├── presentation     → Interfaz gráfica y controladores
./README.md:45:└── resources        → Recursos (imágenes, estilos)
./README.md:47:Domain
./README.md:49:Contiene las entidades principales del juego:
./README.md:51:* `Game`
./README.md:52:* `Board`
./README.md:53:* `Player`
./README.md:54:* `Tile`
./README.md:55:* `Carpet`
./README.md:56:* `Assam`
./README.md:58:Aquí se define la lógica central y reglas del juego.
./README.md:60:----------------------------------------------------
./README.md:62:Application
./README.md:64:Gestiona los casos de uso del sistema:
./README.md:66:* `GameEngine`: Controla el flujo del juego
./README.md:67:* `StatsService`: Manejo de estadísticas
./README.md:69:Infrastructure
./README.md:71:Encargada de la persistencia:
./README.md:73:* Conexión a base de datos
./README.md:74:* Repositorios (`JdbcUserRepository`)
./README.md:76:Presentation
./README.md:78:Contiene la interfaz gráfica:
./README.md:80:* Pantallas (Login, Registro, Lobby, Juego)
./README.md:81:* Controladores que conectan la UI con la lógica del sistema
./README.md:83:Resources
./README.md:85:Incluye:
./README.md:87:* Imágenes del juego (tablero, personajes, alfombras)
./README.md:88:* Archivos de estilos
./README.md:89:------------------------------------------------------------
./README.md:90:Funcionalidades
./README.md:92:* Registro e inicio de sesión de usuarios
./README.md:93:* Sistema de lobby para gestión de partidas
./README.md:94:* Representación gráfica del tablero
./README.md:95:* Movimiento del personaje Assam
./README.md:96:* Colocación de alfombras
./README.md:97:* Sistema de turnos
./README.md:98:* Persistencia de datos de usuarios
./README.md:100:---
./README.md:101:Tecnologías utilizadas
./README.md:103:* Java
./README.md:104:* Maven
./README.md:105:* base de datos H2
./README.md:106:* Git & GitHub
./README.md:108:---
